item.title: 有趣的输出缓冲区问题！ | interesting output buffer question!
item.description:
要更新，这是给我的说明。

从 jyy 那里，我得到了一个非常有趣的问题。

像这样的感觉，这里有一些代码：

 #include <stdio.h> #include <unistd.h> #include <sys/wait.h>
int main ( int argc , char * argv []) { int n = 2 ; for ( int i = 0 ; i < n ; i ++ ) { fork (); printf ( "Hello \n " ); } for ( int i = 0 ; i < n ; i ++ ) { wait ( NULL ); } }

如果我们运行这个程序，我们可以得到这个结果：

 //gcc fork-printf.c && ./a.out Hello Hello Hello Hello Hello Hello

有六个“你好”

但是当我们使用管道命令时，像这样：

 //gcc fork-printf.c && ./a.out | cat Hello Hello Hello Hello Hello Hello Hello Hello

我们会得到八个“你好”。

为什么

在 linux 中，当我们使用标准输出时：

对于termianl：linux使用line buffer ，如果遇到字符\n ，linux使用系统调用输出所有缓冲区。

对于管道/文件：linux 使用full buffer ，当缓冲区大小达到 4096B 时，（除了我们调用fflush(stdout) ）。

条件Ⅰ，使用行缓冲，直接输出； 否则，对于条件Ⅱ，使用full buffer。

Ivad Yves HABIMANA - Ivad Yves HABIMANA - May 14 5月14日



原文:

to be update, this is a note for me.

From jyy,I get a very interesting problem.

Sence like this,here are some code:

#include <stdio.h>
#include <unistd.h>
#include <sys/wait.h>

int main(int argc, char *argv[]) { int n = 2; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { fork(); printf("Hello\n"); } for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { wait(NULL); }
}

if we run this programe, we can get this result:

//gcc fork-printf.c && ./a.out
Hello
Hello
Hello
Hello
Hello
Hello

there has six 'hello'

but when we use the pipe command,like this:

//gcc fork-printf.c && ./a.out | cat
Hello
Hello
Hello
Hello
Hello
Hello
Hello
Hello

we will get eight 'hello'.

why

In linux, when we use stdout:

for termianl: linux use line buffer, if meet character \n, linux use system calling to output all buffer.

for pipe/file: linux use full buffer, when the size of buffer is up to 4096B, (except that we call fflush(stdout)).

for conition Ⅰ，use line buffer, directly output; otherwise, for condition Ⅱ, use full buffer.

Ivad Yves HABIMANA - May 14
item.author: iqium

item.title: Go Echo API 服务器开发 | Go Echo API Server Development
item.description:
基于最低配置启动项目创建一个简单的api服务器，功能如下

  • 通过sql-migrate进行数据库迁移
  • 来自gorm的应用程序的数据库操作
  • 为每个生产和开发环境切换配置文件
  • 用户认证中间件

此外，假设Firebase 身份验证用于用户身份验证，MySQL 用于数据库

我们做什么

两个 API，一个用于检索博客文章列表，另一个用于更新已发布的文章。 任何人都可以访问列出文章的 API，更新文章的 API 只能由发布文章的人访问。

准备

设置

克隆基础项目

$ git clone https://github.com/nrikiji/go-echo-sample

编辑数据库连接信息以匹配您的环境 config.yml 

 development : dialect : mysql datasource : root:@tcp(localhost:3306)/go-echo-example?charset=utf8mb4&collation=utf8mb4_general_ci&parseTime=true dir : migrations table : migrations ...

发布表创建

$ sql-migrate -config config.yml create_posts $ vi migrations/xxxxxxx-create_posts.sql -- +migrate Up CREATE TABLE ` posts ` ( ` id ` bigint ( 20 ) unsigned NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT, ` user_id ` bigint ( 20 ) unsigned NOT NULL, ` title ` varchar ( 64 ) COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL, ` body ` text COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY ( ` id ` ) , FOREIGN KEY ( ` user_id ` ) REFERENCES ` users ` ( ` id ` ) ) ENGINE = InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET = utf8mb4 COLLATE = utf8mb4_unicode_ci ; $ sql-migrate up -env development -config config.yml $ sql-migrate up -env test -config config.yml

此外，用户表的迁移文件包含在基础项目中（只有 id、name 和 firebase_uid 的简单表）

注册虚拟数据

从 firebase 控制台创建一个具有电子邮件地址 + 密码身份验证的用户，并获取 Web 的 API 密钥（项目设置 > 常规中的 Web API 密钥）。

此外，将用于使用 Firebase Admin SDK（Project Settings > Service Account 中的 Firebase Admin SDK）的私钥添加到项目的根目录。 从 API 获取注册用户的 localId（Firebase 用户 ID）和 idToken。 这次直接请求firebase登录API获取idToken和localId

 $ curl 'https://identitytoolkit.googleapis.com/v1/accounts:signInWithPassword?key=APIキー' \ -h 'Content-Type: application/json' \ -d '{"email": "foo@example.com", "password": "password", "returnSecureToken":true}' | jq { "localId" : "xxxxxxxxxxxxxxx" , "idToken" : "xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx" , } }

使用获取的 localId 在 DB 中注册一个用户。

 insert into users ( id , firebase_uid , name , created_at , updated_at ) values ( 1 , "xxxxxxxxxxxxxxx" , "user1" , now (), now ()); insert into posts ( user_id , title , body , created_at , updated_at ) values ( 1 , "title1" , "body1" , now (), now ()), ( 2 , "title2" , "body2" , now (), now ());

现在我们已准备好进行开发

实现数据操作部分

准备一个模型，该模型表示从 DB 检索到的记录。

模型/post.go

 package model type Post struct { ID uint `gorm: "primaryKey" json: "id"` UserID uint `json: "user_id"` User User `json: "user"` Title string `json: "title"` Body string `json: "body"` }

使用 gorm 将方法添加到存储中以从数据库中检索和更新。 由于我们在基础项目中有一个 UserStore，所以这次我们添加了 AllPosts 和 UpdatePost 方法

商店/post.go

 package store import ( "errors" . "go-echo-starter/model" "gorm.io/gorm" ) func ( us * UserStore ) AllPosts () ([] model . Post , error ) { var p [] model . Post err := us . db . Preload ( "User" ) . Find ( & p ) . Error if err ! = nil { if errors . Is ( err , gorm . ErrRecordNotFound ) { return p , nil } return nil , err } return p , nil } func ( us * UserStore ) UpdatePost ( post * model . Post ) error { return us . db . Model ( post ) . Updates ( post ) . Error }

实现采集 API

实现从商店获取模型并在请求时以 json 格式返回响应的部分。

执行

处理程序/post.go

 package handler Import ( "go-echo-starter/model" "net/http" "net/http" "github.com/labstack/echo/v4" ) type postsResponse struct { ( type postsResponse struct ) posts [] model . Post `json: "posts"` } func ( h * Handler ) getPosts ( c echo . Context ) error { . posts , err := h . userStore . AllPosts () if err ! = nil { return err return c . JSON ( http . StatusOK , postsResponse { Posts : posts })) }

当使用路由中名为 /posts 的路径发出 GET 请求时调用处理程序

处理程序/routes.go

 package handler Import ( "go-echo-starter/middleware" "github.com/labstack/echo/v4" ) func ( h * Handler ) Register ( api * echo . Group ){ . ... api . GET ( "/posts" , h . getPosts ) }

检查操作

$ go run server.go ... $ curl http://localhost:8000/api/posts | jq { "posts" : [ { "id" : 1, "user_id" : 1, "user" : { "id" : 1, "name" : "user1" , } , "title" : "title1" , "body" : "body1" , } , } , "title" : "title1" , "body" : "body1" , } }

写测试

准备两个带有夹具的测试数据

固定装置/posts.yml

 - id : 1 user_id : 1 title : " Title1" body : " Body1" - id : 2 user_id : 2 title : " Title2" body : " Body2"

为处理程序编写测试。 在这里我们测试没有错误，并且项目数匹配。

处理程序/post_test.go

 package handler import ( "encoding/json" "net/http" "net/http/httptest" "testing" "github.com/labstack/echo/v4" "github.com/stretchr/testify/assert" ) func TestGetPosts ( t * testing . T ) { setup () req := httptest . NewRequest ( echo . GET , "/api/posts" , nil ) rec := httptest . NewRecorder () c := e . NewContext ( req , rec ) assert . NoError ( t , h . getPosts ( c )) if assert . Equal ( t , http . StatusOK , rec . Code ) { var res postsResponse err := json . Unmarshal ( rec . Body . Bytes (), & res ) assert . NoError ( t , err ) assert . Equal ( t , 2 , len ( res . Posts )) } }

运行测试

$ cd handler $ go test -run TestGetPosts ... ok go-echo-starter/handler 1.380s

实现更新 API

应用auth 中间件进行身份验证，以防止其他人更新他们的帖子。 这个中间件所做的就是从 http 头Authorization: Bearer xxxxx中设置的 firebase idToken 中获取 firebase 用户 id，使用 UID 作为 key 搜索 users 表，并将结果设置在 result is set in context 中。

在处理程序中，如果可以从上下文中检索到用户，则认证成功； 如果不是，则身份验证失败。

 user := context . Get ( "user" ) if user == nil { // authentication fails } else { // authentication succeeded }

执行

处理程序/post.go

 type postResponse struct { { type postResponse struct post model . Post `json: "post"` . } type updatePostRequest struct { { { type updatePostRequest title string `json: "title" validate: "required"` body string `json: "body" validate: "required"` } func ( h * Handler ) updatePost ( c echo . Context ) error { // get user information // get user information u := c . Get ( "user" ) if u == nil { return c . JSON ( http . StatusForbidden , nil ) user := u . ( * model . User ) // get article id , _ := strconv . Atoi ( c . Param ( "id" )) post , err := h . userStore . FindPostByID ( id ) if err ! = nil { { . return c . JSON ( http . StatusInternalServerError , nil ) } else if post == nil { return c . JSON ( http . StatusNotFound , nil ) } // if it is someone else's post, consider it as unauthorized access if post . UserID ! = user . ID { { { if post . UserID ! = user . ID { { { if post . UserID ! return c . JSON ( http . StatusForbidden , nil ) } params := & updatePostRequest {} if err := c . Bind ( params ); err ! = nil { { . return c . JSON ( http . StatusInternalServerError , nil ) } // Validation if err := c . Validate ( params ); err ! = nil { { if err := c . Validate ( params ); err ! return c . JSON ( http . StatusBadRequest , ae . NewValidationError ( err , ae . ValidationMessages { "Title" : { "required" : "Please enter a title" }, "Body" : { "required" : "Please enter a body" }, }), ) } // Update data post . Title = params . Post . Body = params . Body if err := h . userStore . UpdatePost ( post ); err ! = nil { . return c . JSON ( http . StatusInternalServerError , nil ) } return c . JSON ( http . StatusOK , postResponse { Post : * post })) }

可以使用 go-playground/validator 的 ( https://github.com/go-playground/validator/blob/master/translations/ja/ja.go ) 功能完成验证，它允许您显示默认的多语言显示错误消息. 但是，这个应用程序并没有使用它，而是定义了一个以字段名称和验证规则名称为键的映射，并使用显示固定消息。

 if err := c . Validate ( params ); err ! = nil { return c . JSON ( http . StatusBadRequest , ae . NewValidationError ( err , ae . ValidationMessages { "Title" : { "required" : "required Title." }, "Body" : { "required" : "required Body" }, }), ) }

接下来，在路径 /posts 中发出 PATCH 请求时调用您创建的处理程序

处理程序/routes.go

 func ( h * Handler ) Register ( api * echo . Group ) { Auth := middleware . AuthMiddleware ( h . authStore , h . userStore ) ... api . PATCH ( "/posts/:id" , h . updatePost , auth ) }

操作确认

将上面得到的firebase idToken放到http头中，查看运行情况。

 $ go run server.go ... $ curl -X PATCH -H "Content-Type: application/json" \f rz -H "Authorization: Bearer xxxxxxxxxxxxxx" $ curl -d '{"title": "NewTitle", "body": "NewBody1"}' \ http://localhost:8000/api/posts/1 | jq { "post" : { "id" : 1, "title" : "NewTitle" , "body" : "NewBody1" , } } }

在尝试更新其他人的文章时检查错误

$ curl -X PATCH -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer xxxxxxxxxxxxxx" \} -d '{"title": "NewTitle", "body": "NewBody1"}' \ http://localhost:8000/api/posts/2 -v ... HTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden ...

写作测试

处理程序测试您可以更新自己的文章，但不能更新其他人的文章。

更新你自己的文章

处理程序/post_test.go

 func TestUpdatePostSuccess ( t * testing . T ) { setup () reqJSON := `{"title":"NewTitle", "body":"NewBody"}` authMiddleware := middleware . AuthMiddleware ( h . authStore , h . userStore ) req := httptest . NewRequest ( echo . PATCH , "/api/posts/:id" , strings . NewReader ( reqJSON )) req . Header . Set ( echo . HeaderContentType , echo . MIMEApplicationJSON ) req . Header . Set ( echo . HeaderAuthorization , "Bearer: ValidToken1" ) rec := httptest . NewRecorder () c := e . NewContext ( req , rec ) c . SetPath ( "/api/posts/:id" ) c . SetParamNames ( "id" ) c . SetParamValues ( "1" ) err := authMiddleware ( func ( c echo . Context ) error { return h . updatePost ( c ) })( c ) assert . NoError ( t , err ) if assert . Equal ( t , http . StatusOK , rec . Code ) { var res postResponse err := json . Unmarshal ( rec . Body . Bytes (), & res ) assert . NoError ( t , err ) assert . Equal ( t , "NewTitle" , res . Post . Title ) assert . Equal ( t , "NewBody" , res . Post . Body ) } }

使用基础项目中准备的模拟方法。

 func ( f * fakeAuthClient ) VerifyIDToken ( context context . Context , token string ) ( * auth . Token , error ) { var uid string if token == "ValidToken" { uid = "ValidUID" return & auth . Token { UID : uid }, nil } else if token == "ValidToken1" { uid = "ValidUID1" return & auth . Token { UID : uid }, nil } else { return nil , errors . New ( "Invalid Token" ) } }

试图更新别人的文章。

处理程序/post_test.go

 func TestUpdatePostForbidden ( t * testing . T ) { setup () reqJSON := `{"title":"NewTitle", "body":"NewBody"}` authMiddleware := middleware . AuthMiddleware ( h . authStore , h . userStore ) req := httptest . NewRequest ( echo . PATCH , "/api/posts/:id" , strings . NewReader ( reqJSON )) req . Header . Set ( echo . HeaderContentType , echo . MIMEApplicationJSON ) req . Header . Set ( echo . HeaderAuthorization , "Bearer: ValidToken1" ) rec := httptest . NewRecorder () c := e . NewContext ( req , rec ) c . SetPath ( "/api/posts/:id" ) c . SetParamNames ( "id" ) c . SetParamValues ( "2" ) err := authMiddleware ( func ( c echo . Context ) error { return h . updatePost ( c ) })( c ) assert . NoError ( t , err ) assert . Equal ( t , http . StatusForbidden , rec . Code ) }

测试运行

$ go test -run TestUpdatePostSuccess・・・ok go-echo-starter/handler 1.380s $ go test -run TestUpdatePostForbidden・・・ok go-echo-starter/handler 1.380s

结论

我们这次制作的样品
https://github.com/nrikiji/go-echo-sample/tree/blog-example https://github.com/nrikiji/go-echo-sample/tree/blog-example



原文:

Create a simple api server based on a minimum configuration start project with the following functions

  • db migration by sql-migrate
  • db operation from apps by gorm
  • Input check by go-playground/validator
  • Switching of configuration files for each production and development environment
  • User authentication middleware

https://github.com/nrikiji/go-echo-sample

Also, assume Firebase Authentication for user authentication and MySQL for database

What we make

Two APIs, one to retrieve a list of blog posts and the other to update posted posts. The API to list articles can be accessed by anyone, and the API to update articles can only be accessed by the person who posted the article.

Prepare

Setup

Clone the base project

$ git clone https://github.com/nrikiji/go-echo-sample

Edit database connection information to match your environment config.yml

development: dialect: mysql datasource: root:@tcp(localhost:3306)/go-echo-example?charset=utf8mb4&collation=utf8mb4_general_ci&parseTime=true dir: migrations table: migrations
...

posts table creation

$ sql-migrate -config config.yml create_posts $ vi migrations/xxxxxxx-create_posts.sql
-- +migrate Up
CREATE TABLE `posts` ( `id` bigint(20) unsigned NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT, `user_id` bigint(20) unsigned NOT NULL, `title` varchar(64) COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL, `body` text COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (`id`), FOREIGN KEY (`user_id`) REFERENCES `users` (`id`)
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci; $ sql-migrate up -env development -config config.yml $ sql-migrate up -env test -config config.yml

Also, the migration file for the users table is included in the base project (simple table with only id, name and firebase_uid)

Register dummy data

Create a user with email address + password authentication from the firebase console and obtain an API key for the web (Web API key in Project Settings > General).

Also, add the private key for using Firebase Admin SDK (Firebase Admin SDK in Project Settings > Service Account) to the root of the project. (In this case, the file name is firebase_secret_key.json.

Obtain the localId (Firebase user ID) and idToken of the registered user from the API. localId is set in users.firebase_uid and idToken is set in the http header when requesting the API.

This time, request directly to firebase login API to get idToken and localId

$ curl 'https://identitytoolkit.googleapis.com/v1/accounts:signInWithPassword?key=APIキー' \
-h 'Content-Type: application/json' \
-d '{"email": "foo@example.com", "password": "password", "returnSecureToken":true}' | jq { "localId": "xxxxxxxxxxxxxxx", "idToken": "xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx", }
}

Register a user in DB with the obtained localId.

insert into users (id, firebase_uid, name, created_at, updated_at) values (1, "xxxxxxxxxxxxxxx", "user1", now(), now()); insert into posts (user_id, title, body, created_at, updated_at) values (1, "title1", "body1", now(), now()), (2, "title2", "body2", now(), now());

Now we are ready for development

Implement the data manipulation part

Prepare a model that represents records retrieved from DB.

model/post.go

package model type Post struct { ID uint `gorm: "primaryKey" json: "id"` UserID uint `json: "user_id"` User User `json: "user"` Title string `json: "title"` Body string `json: "body"`
}

Use gorm to add methods to the store to retrieve from and update the DB. Since we have a UserStore in the base project, we add the AllPosts and UpdatePost methods to it this time

store/post.go

package store import ( "errors". "go-echo-starter/model" "gorm.io/gorm"
) func (us *UserStore) AllPosts() ([]model.Post, error) { var p []model.Post err := us.db.Preload("User").Find(&p).Error if err ! = nil { if errors.Is(err, gorm.ErrRecordNotFound) { return p, nil } return nil, err } return p, nil
} func (us *UserStore) UpdatePost(post *model.Post) error { return us.db.Model(post).Updates(post).Error
}

Implementing the acquisition API

Implement the part that acquires the model from the store and returns the response in json when requested.

Implementation

handler/post.go

package handler Import ( "go-echo-starter/model" "net/http" "net/http" "github.com/labstack/echo/v4"
) type postsResponse struct { (type postsResponse struct) posts []model.Post `json: "posts"`
} func (h *Handler) getPosts(c echo.Context) error {. posts, err := h.userStore.AllPosts() if err ! = nil { return err return c.JSON(http.StatusOK, postsResponse{Posts: posts}))
}

Call the handler when a GET request is made with a path named /posts in a route

handler/routes.go

package handler Import ( "go-echo-starter/middleware" "github.com/labstack/echo/v4"
) func (h *Handler) Register(api *echo.Group){. ... api.GET("/posts", h.getPosts)
}

Check operation 

$ go run server.go
... $ curl http://localhost:8000/api/posts | jq
{ "posts": [ { "id": 1, "user_id": 1, "user": { "id": 1, "name": "user1", }, "title": "title1", "body": "body1", }, }, "title": "title1", "body": "body1", }
}

write test

Prepare two test data with fixtures

fixtures/posts.yml

- id: 1 user_id: 1 title: "Title1" body: "Body1" - id: 2 user_id: 2 title: "Title2" body: "Body2"

Write tests for the handler. Here we test that there is no error, and that the number of items matches.

handler/post_test.go

package handler import ( "encoding/json" "net/http" "net/http/httptest" "testing" "github.com/labstack/echo/v4" "github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
) func TestGetPosts(t *testing.T) { setup() req := httptest.NewRequest(echo.GET, "/api/posts", nil) rec := httptest.NewRecorder() c := e.NewContext(req, rec) assert.NoError(t, h.getPosts(c)) if assert.Equal(t, http.StatusOK, rec.Code) { var res postsResponse err := json.Unmarshal(rec.Body.Bytes(), &res) assert.NoError(t, err) assert.Equal(t, 2, len(res.Posts)) }
}

Run a test

$ cd handler
$ go test -run TestGetPosts
...
ok go-echo-starter/handler 1.380s

Implement update API

Apply auth middleware for authentication to prevent others from updating their posts. What this middleware does is to get the firebase user id from the firebase idToken set in the http header Authorization: Bearer xxxxx, search the users table using the UID as a key, and set the result in The result is set in context.

In the handler, if the user can be retrieved from the context, authentication succeeds; if not, authentication fails.

user := context.Get("user")
if user == nil { // authentication fails
} else { // authentication succeeded
}

Implementation

handler/post.go

type postResponse struct { { type postResponse struct post model.Post `json: "post"`.
} type updatePostRequest struct { { { type updatePostRequest title string `json: "title" validate: "required"` body string `json: "body" validate: "required"`
} func (h *Handler) updatePost(c echo.Context) error { // get user information // get user information u := c.Get("user") if u == nil { return c.JSON(http.StatusForbidden, nil) user := u.(*model.User) // get article id, _ := strconv.Atoi(c.Param("id")) post, err := h.userStore.FindPostByID(id) if err ! = nil { {. return c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, nil) } else if post == nil { return c.JSON(http.StatusNotFound, nil) } // if it is someone else's post, consider it as unauthorized access if post.UserID ! = user.ID { { { if post.UserID ! = user.ID { { { if post.UserID ! return c.JSON(http.StatusForbidden, nil) } params := &updatePostRequest{} if err := c.Bind(params); err ! = nil { {. return c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, nil) } // Validation if err := c.Validate(params); err ! = nil { { if err := c.Validate(params); err ! return c.JSON( http.StatusBadRequest, ae.NewValidationError(err, ae.ValidationMessages{ "Title": {"required": "Please enter a title"}, "Body": {"required": "Please enter a body"}, }), ) } // Update data post.Title = params. Post.Body = params.Body if err := h.userStore.UpdatePost(post); err ! = nil { . return c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, nil) } return c.JSON(http.StatusOK, postResponse{Post: *post}))
}

Validation can be done using go-playground/validator's (https://github.com/go-playground/validator/blob/master/translations/ja/ja.go) functionality, which allows you to display default multilingual display of error messages. However, this app does not use it, but instead defines a map keyed by field name and validation rule name, and uses display fixed messages.

if err := c.Validate(params); err ! = nil { return c.JSON( http.StatusBadRequest, ae.NewValidationError(err, ae.ValidationMessages{ "Title": {"required": "required Title."}, "Body": {"required": "required Body"}, }), )
}

Next, call the handler you created when a PATCH request is made in routes with the path /posts

handler/routes.go

func (h *Handler) Register(api *echo.Group) { Auth := middleware.AuthMiddleware(h.authStore, h.userStore) ... api.PATCH("/posts/:id", h.updatePost, auth)
}

Confirmation of operation

Put the firebase idToken obtained above in the http header and check the operation.

$ go run server.go
... $ curl -X PATCH -H "Content-Type: application/json" \frz
-H "Authorization: Bearer xxxxxxxxxxxxxx" $ curl
-d '{"title": "NewTitle", "body": "NewBody1"}' \
http://localhost:8000/api/posts/1 | jq { "post": { "id": 1, "title": "NewTitle", "body": "NewBody1", } }
}

Checking for errors when trying to update someone else's article

$ curl -X PATCH -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
-H "Authorization: Bearer xxxxxxxxxxxxxx" \}
-d '{"title": "NewTitle", "body": "NewBody1"}' \
http://localhost:8000/api/posts/2 -v

...
HTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden
...

Writing Tests

Handler tests that you can update your own articles, but not others'.

Update your own article

handler/post_test.go

func TestUpdatePostSuccess(t *testing.T) { setup() reqJSON := `{"title":"NewTitle", "body":"NewBody"}` authMiddleware := middleware.AuthMiddleware(h.authStore, h.userStore) req := httptest.NewRequest(echo.PATCH, "/api/posts/:id", strings.NewReader(reqJSON)) req.Header.Set(echo.HeaderContentType, echo.MIMEApplicationJSON) req.Header.Set(echo.HeaderAuthorization, "Bearer: ValidToken1") rec := httptest.NewRecorder() c := e.NewContext(req, rec) c.SetPath("/api/posts/:id") c.SetParamNames("id") c.SetParamValues("1") err := authMiddleware(func(c echo.Context) error { return h.updatePost(c) })(c) assert.NoError(t, err) if assert.Equal(t, http.StatusOK, rec.Code) { var res postResponse err := json.Unmarshal(rec.Body.Bytes(), &res) assert.NoError(t, err) assert.Equal(t, "NewTitle", res.Post.Title) assert.Equal(t, "NewBody", res.Post.Body) }
}

test returns a fixed user id by idToken for the conversion of idToken to Firebase user id, which is done by the authentication middleware.　Use the mock method prepared in base project.

func (f *fakeAuthClient) VerifyIDToken(context context.Context, token string) (*auth.Token, error) { var uid string if token == "ValidToken" { uid = "ValidUID" return &auth.Token{UID: uid}, nil } else if token == "ValidToken1" { uid = "ValidUID1" return &auth.Token{UID: uid}, nil } else { return nil, errors.New("Invalid Token") }
}

Trying to update someone else's article.

handler/post_test.go

func TestUpdatePostForbidden(t *testing.T) { setup() reqJSON := `{"title":"NewTitle", "body":"NewBody"}` authMiddleware := middleware.AuthMiddleware(h.authStore, h.userStore) req := httptest.NewRequest(echo.PATCH, "/api/posts/:id", strings.NewReader(reqJSON)) req.Header.Set(echo.HeaderContentType, echo.MIMEApplicationJSON) req.Header.Set(echo.HeaderAuthorization, "Bearer: ValidToken1") rec := httptest.NewRecorder() c := e.NewContext(req, rec) c.SetPath("/api/posts/:id") c.SetParamNames("id") c.SetParamValues("2") err := authMiddleware(func(c echo.Context) error { return h.updatePost(c) })(c) assert.NoError(t, err) assert.Equal(t, http.StatusForbidden, rec.Code)
}

test run

$ go test -run TestUpdatePostSuccess
・・・
ok go-echo-starter/handler 1.380s $ go test -run TestUpdatePostForbidden
・・・
ok      go-echo-starter/handler 1.380s

Conclusion

Sample we made this time
https://github.com/nrikiji/go-echo-sample/tree/blog-example
item.author: nrikiji

item.title: C++ 概念在行动 | C++ Concepts In Action
item.description:
模板元编程是一个复杂的话题，即使是经验丰富的老手也很难过。 我发现了这个特性的一个有趣用法，以确保我可以编写一个重载的流插入运算符，该运算符可以输出支持基于范围的迭代的 C++ 容器（基本上是具有 begin 和 end 方法的类型）。

 #include <iostream> #include <unordered_map> #include <array> #include <vector> using namespace std; template<typename ContainerType> concept SupportsRangeBasedFor = requires(ContainerType container) { container.begin(); container.end(); }; template<typename KeyType, typename ValueType> ostream& operator<<(ostream& os, const pair<KeyType, ValueType>& p) { os << p.first << "->" << p.second; return os; } ostream& operator<<(ostream& os, SupportsRangeBasedFor auto&& container) { os << "[ "; for (const auto& element : container) { os << element << ' '; } os << ']' << endl; return os; } int main() { cout << vector<int>{1,2,3} << endl; cout << array<int,3>{4,5,6} << endl; cout << unordered_map<int,int>{{8,9},{10,11},{11,12}} << endl; struct Foo {}; cout << Foo {} << endl; // Compiler Error: No overload for operator<< that accepts Foo return 0; }

运行这个程序会产生这个输出：

 ~/code/cpp/concepts > ./concept [ 1 2 3 ] [ 4 5 6 ] [ 11->12 10->11 8->9 ]

因此，我们有一个可以支持任何具有 begin 和 end 方法的容器的重载。 这不是魔法，你可以在没有SupportsRangeBasedFor概念的情况下让它工作，但魔法是这个概念阻止发生的事情：

如果没有对模板参数的约束，编译器会很乐意为Foo选择operator<<重载，并且由于Foo没有begin和end方法这一事实，您会得到一个难以理解的错误。 您将看到这种性质的错误：

 concept.cpp:22:30: error: invalid range expression of type 'Foo'; no viable 'begin' function available for (const auto& element : container) { ^ ~~~~~~~~~ concept.cpp:35:10: note: in instantiation of function template specialization 'operator<<<Foo>' requested here cout << Foo {} << endl;

不仅如此，编译器将匹配任何ostream.operator<<(...)调用此重载，因为模板参数将匹配所有类型。

该概念表达的类型约束明确禁止编译器为您不希望支持的类型选择模板函数。 这是一个非常简洁的功能，我希望在我未来的开发中广泛使用它来限制我的实现应该选择的类型:)



原文:

C++20 has a new feature called Concept that allows developers to specify constraints on template parameters. Template meta programming is a complex topic that stumps even seasoned veterans. I found an interesting usage of this feature to ensure that I can write an overloaded stream insertion operator that can output C++ containers that support range based iteration (basically types that have begin and end methods).

#include <iostream>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <array>
#include <vector>
using namespace std;

template<typename ContainerType>
concept SupportsRangeBasedFor = requires(ContainerType container) {
    container.begin();
    container.end();
};

template<typename KeyType, typename ValueType>
ostream& operator<<(ostream& os, const pair<KeyType, ValueType>& p) {
    os << p.first << "->" << p.second;
    return os;
}

ostream& operator<<(ostream& os, SupportsRangeBasedFor auto&& container) {
    os << "[ ";
    for (const auto& element : container) {
        os << element << ' ';
    }
    os << ']' << endl;
    return os;
}

int main()
{
    cout << vector<int>{1,2,3} << endl;
    cout << array<int,3>{4,5,6} << endl;
    cout << unordered_map<int,int>{{8,9},{10,11},{11,12}} << endl;
    struct Foo {};
    cout << Foo {} << endl; // Compiler Error: No overload for operator<< that accepts Foo
    return 0;
}

Running this program produces this output:

~/code/cpp/concepts > ./concept
[ 1 2 3 ]

[ 4 5 6 ]

[ 11->12 10->11 8->9 ]

So, we have a single overload that can support any container that has a begin and end method. This is not magic, you could have got this to work without SupportsRangeBasedFor concept, but the magic is what the concept prevents from happening:

Without the constraint on the template parameter, compiler would have happily chosen operator<< overload for Foo and you would get an indecipherable blob of errors caused by the fact that Foo doesn't have begin and end methods. You will see errors of this nature:

concept.cpp:22:30: error: invalid range expression of type 'Foo'; no viable 'begin' function available
    for (const auto& element : container) {
                             ^ ~~~~~~~~~
concept.cpp:35:10: note: in instantiation of function template specialization 'operator<<<Foo>' requested here
    cout << Foo {} << endl;

Not only that, the compiler would match any ostream.operator<<(...) call to this overload because the template parameter will match all types.

The type constraint expressed by the concept explicitly forbids the compiler from picking up your template function for types that you don't wish to support. This is a pretty neat feature and I hope to be using this quite widely in my future development to limit the types that my implementation should be chosen for :)
item.author: mdh81

item.title: 让开发人员成为生产力绝地的 7 个简单技巧 ⚡️ | 7 Easy Hacks for Developers to become a Productivity Jedi ⚡️
item.description:
每个人都想用最少的努力完成很多事情。 这篇文章包含 7 个这样久经考验的技巧，可以帮助您在少工作的同时取得更多成就！

我们生活在一个信息丰富的世界里，使用谷歌可以在 1 分钟内回答大多数问题，因此试图记住所有内容是没有意义的。

你唯一应该背诵的东西是编程的核心概念，没有它你就不会知道你不知道什么。

例如：如果您尝试重复搜索元素，知道哈希列表将导致快速搜索，这对您有很大帮助。 如何实际使用哈希列表因语言而异，但可以在Google上轻松查找

hash_list = set ( our_list ) for search_element in search_list : if search_element in hash_list : pass # handle element

时间盒是一种将时间分配给特定任务的技术。 这可以让你做深入的工作。 请记住在开始工作之前将手机保持静音。

 8:30 --> 9:00 Emails & Catch up on Slack 9:00 --> 13:00 Working on XYZ project 13:00 --> 14:00 Lunch and break 14:00 --> 15:00 Team meeting 15:00 --> 16:30 Working on XYZ project

如果你的工作是吃青蛙，最好早上第一件事。 如果你必须吃两只青蛙，从较大的一只开始。

老实说，没有人喜欢吃青蛙，即使是比喻性的。 但是，在一天的早些时候完成待办事项中的艰巨任务有一个巨大的优势：你放弃了无法完成任务的感觉（这会慢慢吞噬你的灵魂），换取随之而来的剧烈行动痛苦当天晚些时候令人难以置信的骄傲！

能量水平全天变化。 花几天时间逐小时记录您的能量水平，并制定适合您身体的时间表。

与你的身体一起工作而不是反对它的结果，对于大多数人来说都是惊人的，而且生产力将简直是天翻地覆！

它提倡通过重复使用和回收您已经完成的工作来节省时间。

重复本质上是低效的。 这种方法的真正强大之处在于，它不仅限于编码，还可以应用于电子邮件、自动支付卡等等！

研究表明，大约 2% 的人可以有效地执行多项任务。 每个人都认为他们是神话般的百分之二的人是令人难以置信的。

虽然多任务处理并没有真正同时处理多个任务，但实际上你在多个任务之间不断切换，错过了深度工作，并且有效地完成了所有这些工作。

每天完成您的每日目标，您都会在日历中添加一个❌。

承诺的最初几天通常是最难的——但你做出改变的动力会让你度过难关。

随着链条越来越长，不打破你的连胜成为你想要退出的时代的首要任务，从而改变生活！

这就是所有的人！ 现在，您拥有成为生产力之神的工具！

世界等你来拿！

需要顶级前端开发自由职业者来消除您的开发困境？ 在Upwork上联系我

想看看我在做什么？ 查看我的个人网站和GitHub

想要连接？ 在LinkedIn上联系我

我是一名自由职业者，将于 2022 年年中开始担任Digital Nomad 。 想赶上旅程吗？ 在Instagram 上关注我

关注我的博客，了解Dev上的每周新花絮

常问问题

这些是我得到的一些常见问题。 所以，我希望这个常见问题解答部分能解决您的问题。



原文:

Everyone wants to get a lot done while putting in the least amount of effort. This article contains 7 such tried & tested hacks that will help you achieve more while working less!

1. Don't Memorize Google-able info!🔎

We live in a world of abundant information, and most questions can be answered within 1 minute using Google, so there is no point in trying to memorize everything.

The only things you should be learning by heart are the core concepts of programming, without which you would not know what you don't know.

Eg: If you are trying to repeatedly search for elements, knowing a hash list will result in blazing fast searches that will help you a lot. How to actually use a hash list varies from language to language, but can easily be looked up on Google

hash_list = set(our_list) for search_element in search_list: if search_element in hash_list: pass # handle element

2. Use Time Boxing ⌛

Time Boxing is the technique of blocking out & allocating time to a specific task. This allows you to do deep work. Just remember to keep your phone on silent before you start working.

8:30 --> 9:00
Emails & Catch up on Slack

9:00 --> 13:00
Working on XYZ project

13:00 --> 14:00
Lunch and break

14:00 --> 15:00
Team meeting

15:00 --> 16:30
Working on XYZ project

3. Eat the damn Frog! 🐸

If it's your job to eat the frog, best to do it first thing in the morning. If you have to eat two frogs, start with the larger one.

Let's be honest, nobody likes eating frogs, even metaphorical ones. But getting the hard tasks on your to-do done early in the day has a huge advantage: you trade the feeling of being someone who can't get things done (that slowly eats away your soul), for an acute pain of action followed by incredible pride later in the day!

4. Use Biology to your Advantage! 📈

The energy levels vary throughout the day. Take a few days to document your energy levels hour by hour and create a schedule suited to your body.

The results of working with your body instead of against it, will astounding for most people & the productivity will simply go through the roof!

5. Stay DRY! 🏜️

The DRY (Don’t Repeat Yourself) principle is one of the core principles of Programming. It advocates saving time by reusing and recycling work you’ve already done.

Repetition is by its nature inefficient. The DRY Principle encourages you to create workflows and templates to minimize them. The true power of this method comes from the fact, that it is not restricted only to coding, but can be applied to emails, automating your card payments, and much more!

6. Forget Multi-Tasking! 🤹

Research states around two percent of people can multitask effectively. How everyone believes that they are the mythical two percenter is mind-boggling.

While multi-tasking you are not truly handling multiple tasks at the same time, you are actually constantly switching between multiple tasks, missing out on deep work, and effectively doing a mediocre job at all of them.

7. Make the Chain Longer! 🔗

The “Don't Break The Chain” method commits you to complete a daily goal for an extended period. Each day that you complete your daily goal, you add an ❌ to a calendar.

The first few days of commitment are usually the hardest – but your motivation to make a change pulls you through.

As the chain grows longer, not breaking your streak becomes the priority that pulls you through the times when you want to quit, resulting in life-changing improvements!

That's all folks! Now you have the tools in your arsenal to become a productivity god!

The world is up for your taking!

Want to see what I am working on? Check out my Personal Website and GitHub

Follow my blogs for Weekly new Tidbits on Dev

FAQ

These are a few commonly asked questions I get. So, I hope this FAQ section solves your issues.

item.title: 我如何开始数据科学职业？ | How do I start a Data Science Career?
item.description:
数据科学是一个快速发展的领域，并且有很好的机会。 但是你怎么能成为一名数据科学家呢？ 数据科学是一个具有挑战性的领域，但只要具备适当的技能，你就可以胜任。

你可能有很多疑问，比如应该关注哪些技术，Python 最好还是 R？ 编码是开始职业的必要条件吗？ 你需要在技术教育方面毕业吗？ 需要的统计知识水平？ 在本文中，我们将讨论什么是重要的，什么不是。 成为一名数据科学家需要学习和忘记什么。

选择正确的工作角色！

数据科学有许多工作角色，如数据可视化专家、机器学习专家、数据科学家、数据工程师等知名人士。 您可以根据您的教育背景或经验选择工作角色。 如果您仍然感到困惑，请彻底了解该行业。 与专家交谈，深入了解工作职责。 您还可以在考虑工作角色时获得导师的帮助和他们的建议。

开始数据科学培训

在决定了哪个角色对您有利之后，请花一些时间并了解该角色，因为每个角色都有其职责。 角色可能相同，但职责可能因组织而异。 下一步是选择一个好的数据科学培训机构来开始您的数据科学培训。 许多机构提供数据科学培训计划。 始终选择提供安置帮助和职业指导的机构。 在开始程序之前检查评级和评论。 加入课程后，请确保您积极参与，关注所有作业和实时项目，并清除您的所有疑虑。 在完成培训之前，尝试开发您的应用程序或程序并寻求培训师的帮助； 这将帮助您获得信心。

选择一种工具/语言！

对您选择的语言/工具有一个很好的理解总是至关重要的。 没有适当的知识，您就无法最佳地应用它。 实用知识将在您未来的项目中帮助您。 现在下一步是什么工具或语言最好？ 从所有语言中最简单的开始总是更好。 在使用工具时，如果您来自非编码背景，则从基于 GUI 的工具开始，一旦精通它，您就可以尝试编码。 点击这里学习数据科学课程

加入支持小组

确定角色后，开始准备。 首先，加入一个在线支持小组。 支持小组可以帮助您理解事物并让您保持积极性。 开始一个新的职业是可怕的，知道还有其他人经历过你正在经历的事情，这让我松了一口气。 您总是可以通过互联网进行有意义的技术讨论。

实践知识比理论更重要

在接受培训时，重要的是要专注于实际应用，而不仅仅是理论。 它将帮助您更好地理解这个概念。 练习所有作业，处理少量数据集并将它们应用到您正在学习的内容中。 尝试了解已经解决的数据集，这将使您深入了解该主题。

数据库和SQL知识

数据需要放入表格中，作为初学者，您可以使用 CSV 或 excel 文件来表格数据，但它不能是永久的。 数据存储技术和最少的大数据知识将为您的简历增添价值。

沟通技巧

我们大多数人都忘记了沟通技巧对任何工作都很重要。 作为一名数据科学家，您需要具备出色的沟通技巧，因为您将与所有部门合作，收集数据并交付他们需要的东西。 开始专注于沟通技巧并与团队合作将帮助您在工作中成长并使您的任务更容易。

网络

你是数据科学的新手，所以最初更专注于学习和实践。 一旦你确信你拥有工作所需的知识。 开始参观会议和研讨会； 这将帮助您建立一个网络，帮助您以后找到工作。 点击这里学习数据科学课程大纲

结论

我们已经在本文中指出了您开始数据科学职业所需的所有重要事项。 加入一个好的培训机构将在各个方面为您提供帮助。 也是你需要的导师。 世界一流的课程、LMS 访问、实时项目和作业将帮助您掌握该主题。 接受 IIT、ISB 和 IIM 专业人士的培训，并提供安置帮助。



原文:

Data Science is a rapidly growing domain and has good opportunities. But how can you become a Data Scientist? Data Science is a challenging domain, but with the appropriate skill-set, you can run the show.

You might have many doubts like- what techniques to focus on, Is Python best or R? is coding a must to start the career? Do you need to have graduation in technical education? Level of statistics knowledge needed? In this article, we will discuss what is vital and what’s not. What to learn and unlearn to become a Data Scientist.

Choose the job role right!

Data Science has many job roles like Data Visualization expert, Machine Learning expert, Data Scientist, Data Engineer well-known ones. You can pick a job role depending on your educational background or experience. If you are still confused, get a thorough understanding of the industry. Talk to experts and get a deep understanding of the job responsibilities. You can also take the help of a mentor and their suggestions in considering the job role.

Start a Data Science Training

After deciding what role is good for you, dedicate some time and understand the role as every role has its responsibilities. The role might be the same but, responsibilities might differ from one organization to other. The next step is choosing a good Data Science Training Institute to start your Data Science training. Many institutes offer Data Science Training Programs. Always go for an institute that provides placement assistance and career guidance. Check the rating and reviews before starting the program. After joining the course, make sure you participate actively, follow all the assignments and real-time projects and clear all your doubts. Before completing the training, try to develop your app or a program and take help from your trainers; This will help you gain confidence.

Pick a tool/ language!

It is always vital to have a good understanding of the language/tool you chose. Without the proper knowledge, you cannot apply it optimally. Practical knowledge will help you in your future projects. Now the next step is what tool or language is best? It is always better to start with the simplest of all languages. While coming to tools, if you are from a non-coding background start with GUI-based tools, and once well versed with it you can try the coding. Click here to learn data science course

Join a support group

After deciding the role, start your preparation. Firstly, join an online support group. A support group helps you understand things and keeps you motivated. Starting a new career is scary and it is relieving to know that there are others out there who have experienced what you are experiencing. You can always have a meaningful technical discussion over the internet.

Practical knowledge is pivotal than theory

While undergoing training, it is vital to concentrate on practical applications than just theory. It will help you in a better understanding of the concept. Practice all the assignments, work on few data sets and apply them to you’re learning. Try understanding already solved data sets that will provide you an insight into the subject.

Database and SQL knowledge

SQL is the most important basic skill you need to become a Data Science professional. Data needs to be put in tables and as a beginner, you might use CSV or an excel file to table data, but it cannot be permanent. Data storage techniques and minimal Big Data Knowledge will add value to your resume.

Communication Skills

Most of us forget communication skills are important for any job. As a Data Scientist, you need to have exceptional communication skills as you will be working with all the departments collecting data and delivering what they need. Start concentrating on communication skills and working with a team will help you grow in the job and make your tasks easier.

Network

You are new to Data Science, so concentrate more on learning and practicing initially. Once you are confident that you have the required knowledge of the job. Start visiting meetings and seminars; this will help you build a network that will help you in bagging a job later. Click here to learn Data Science Course Syllabus

Conclusion

We have pointed all the vital things in this article that are needed to start your career in Data Science. Joining a good training institute will help you in all aspects. 360DigiTMG is one such place that offers the best training programs and also guides you through your journey. And is also the mentor you need. A world-class curriculum, LMS access, real-time projects, and assignments will help you in mastering the subject. Get trained by IIT, ISB and, IIM professionals with placement assistance.

item.description:
我是一个非常懒惰的人，总是在最小的事情上拖延——打开图像编辑器，制作一个好的博客横幅总是我每次写东西时必须做的“额外”事情。 所以，我制作了一个脚本，可以在几毫秒内为我生成它们！

正如比尔盖茨所说，

原文:

I'm a very lazy person, and always procrastinate on the smallest of things - opening the image editor, and making a good blog banner would always be the "extra" thing I had to do every time I wrote something. So, I made a script that generates them for me in milliseconds!

As Bill Gates says,

always choose a lazy person to do a difficult job because he will find an easy way to do it

The output is fully customizable, as it is 100% generated from code. You can change some values here and there, and boom! Generated. I ran it through all the posts of my blog and it quickly replaced many of the blog posts for me.

Check out the code Here (And ⭐ the post if you liked it!)

I was tired of making banners for my blog posts, so I created a script that does it for me.

Installation

git clone https://github.com/Dhravya/blog-banner-generator.git
cd blog-banner-generator
pip install -r requirements.txt

Config

The default config works well, but if you want your banner to be a different colour, or change the positioning of elements, feel free to edit the config file

Here are the config options (note that this may break the script, because I haven't tested it out much)

BG_COLOR : rgb(r, g, b)

ART_POSITION : Coordinates(x, y, size=(430, 430))

TITLE_POSITION : Coordinates(x, y)

IMG_POSITION : Coordinates(x, y)

FOOTER_POSITION : Coordinates(x, y)

FOOTER : "<your small footer>"

Running the script

You can run the script by using the generate() method of the ImageFactory class

if __name__ == "__main__" generator = ImageFactory() generator.generate( ... # Options here
    )

Generate options:

title, description…

Joao Carlos Sousa do Vale - Apr 19

item.title: 什么是坏代码以及如何处理它 | What is bad code and what to do about it
item.description:
糟糕的代码就像糟糕的修复。 它可以工作，甚至可以工作，但它很糟糕，而且时间不长，它看起来一般，而且要修复它并不容易。 它很混乱，结构难以理解，你不会立即明白作者想说什么。

代码质量差的迹象：

  • 很难在其中导航；

  • 能写成两行的部分写成十行；

  • 通过函数的名称无法理解它的作用（xyz，function，n）；

  • 如果你添加一些东西，其余的将停止工作。

是什么威胁着糟糕代码的项目

乍一看，糟糕的代码并不是一场灾难。 该程序有效，这不是主要的吗？ 事实上，它带来了很多问题，尤其是从长远来看。 这就是为什么：

  • 对低质量代码进行更改需要大量时间且成本高昂，而且随着时间的推移，价格和持续时间只会增加；

  • 添加新东西也很困难；

  • 代码是不可预测的——你永远不知道什么会停止工作以及什么时候停止工作；

  • 如果人们不得不使用这样的杰作，团队精神就会下降（“这到底是谁写的？！”）。

结果，开发速度降低，时间线变长，变更成本越来越高。 如果这对消费者或客户至关重要，那么糟糕的代码可能会掩埋项目。

如何写出好的代码

要编写高质量的代码，您需要知道它是什么。 以下是它的主要品质：

  • 代码易于阅读，结构清晰，没有混淆；

  • 它易于维护：小的变化不会引起再次编写它的愿望；

  • 它可以扩展；

  • 代码已经过测试；

  • 记录在案；

  • 富有成效的。

注释对于使代码易于阅读非常重要。 这不仅会在其他程序员处理您的代码时有所帮助，而且在未来也会对您有所帮助。

以下是改进代码的五个操作。

这是最简单和最重要的准则之一——当您事先与同事达成一致时，如何格式化代码以及如何在某些情况下采取行动。 那么不同程序员编写的代码在视觉上会很相似：你使用别人的代码会更容易，反之亦然。 还有普遍接受的编码标准，甚至编程平台会告诉你如何遵循这些标准。 这在团队工作和单独编写代码时都很重要。

这是在不改变其行为的情况下对程序代码的改造。 也就是说，首先你有一个创造性的过程：你编写以便程序工作，然后你“自己清理”，把代码按顺序排列成想要的样子。 作为奖励，重构有助于重新思考程序的架构决策。

让我们用一个真实的例子来解释。 玩家写信给一款游戏的开发者关于问题，然后突然停止。 起初，开发人员很高兴：一切都非常酷，没有任何问题。 后来发现用户名在游戏中变成了昵称，因此，反馈表停止工作：投诉根本没有发送。 键入（声明数据类型）将有助于避免此类错误并简化维护和重构。

这是您和同事检查彼此的代码并互相提供反馈的时候。 这种做法有助于避免愚蠢的错误和粗心大意。 此外（这也很重要），您知道其他人在做什么，并且您不必复制功能。 一个美妙的奖励 - 这种方法将有助于温和地适应初学者。

这些是运行具有不同参数的代码并检查已知结果的简单程序。 有哪些测试？

  • 对程序的各个部分进行单元测试。

  • 加载器检查程序处理负载的能力。

  • 屏幕截图比较屏幕截图前后的变化，并对变化做出反应。

  • 其他：突变、渗透……

对于测试，您确实需要小心，以免您花费数小时寻找代码中的错误，但问题在于测试本身。 因此，当您对需要做什么和想要得到什么有清晰的结构和理解时，先编写测试然后再编写代码本身是很有用的。

还有其他改进代码的方法（例如，结对编程）。 如果一次使用多种方法可以获得最佳结果：这样更可靠。

当好代码坏时

我们一直在谈论质量代码的重要性，现在我们说的恰恰相反？ 是的。 有时好的、“正确”的代码并不是那么好。
原因很简单：不幸的是，在编程中，您必须在工作质量和速度之间做出选择，而在某些情况下，速度更重要。

要了解速度的重要性，请想象两个开发人员：John 和 Steve。 一是彻底的、迂腐的，二是完全相反的。 两人都想出了制造某种产品的想法。 约翰坐下来，想出架构并开始慢慢地做好它。 并且已经做了六个月了。 史蒂夫在一个月内完成了所有事情，有错误，有错误 - 但立即启动它，找到客户，发布第二个版本......结果，六个月后，当约翰刚刚完成他的精彩产品时，史蒂夫已经拥有很多用户并且该产品的名称已经众所周知。 他修复了第二个版本中的一些错误。

故事的寓意：有时更快地进入市场并占据一席之地比做得完美更重要。

那该怎样呢？ 糟糕的代码是糟糕的，好的代码也不总是好的......重点是：

代码质量是一个参数
我们根据目标而改变。

如果您正在为银行编写应用程序，那么您将花费大量时间进行测试和开发，因为一个错误可能会造成数十万美元的损失。

如果您正在制作一个简单地讲述临时促销并将电子邮件收集到数据库的登录页面，那么速度在这里就已经很重要了。 您知道，一旦促销结束，您的代码将不再需要。 不需要细化和维护它，其他开发者也不会使用它——快速编写并检查它是否有效就足够了，其余的不重要。

这些是创建高质量代码的基本原则：不要忘记标准，回顾你已经编写的内容，不要害怕同事的反馈，不要忘记测试。 但一开始还是值得权衡的：现在质量对你来说很重要，或者你需要尽快完成开发。



原文:

Bad code is like bad repair. It can work and even works, but it’s bad and not for long, it looks so-so and it’s not easy to fix something in it. It is chaotic, has an incomprehensible structure, and you won’t immediately understand what the author wanted to say.

Signs of poor quality code:

  • it is difficult to navigate in it;

  • parts that can be written in two lines are written in ten;

  • by the name of the function it is impossible to understand what it does (xyz, function, n);

  • if you add something to it, the rest will stop working.

What threatens a project with bad code

At first glance, bad code is not a disaster. The program works, isn't that the main thing? In fact, it brings many problems, especially in the long run. And that's why:

  • making changes to low-quality code takes a lot of time and is expensive, and over time, the price and duration only increase;

  • adding something new is also difficult;

  • code is unpredictable - you never know what will stop working and when;

  • team spirit falls if people have to work with such masterpieces ("Who wrote this at all?!").

As a result, the development speed is reduced, the timeline is lengthened, and the cost of changes is growing. If this is critical for the consumer or customer, then bad code can bury the project.

How to write good code

To write quality code, you need to know what it is. Here are its main qualities:

  • the code is easy to read, it has a clear structure, there is no confusion in it;

  • it is easily maintained: small changes do not cause a desire to write it again;

  • it can be expanded;

  • the code has been tested;

  • it is documented;

  • productive.

Comments are really very important to make the code easy to read. This will not only help other programmers when they work on your code, but also you in the future.

Here are five actions that improve the code.

1. Follow coding standards. This is one of the simplest and most important guidelines - when you agree with colleagues in advance how to format the code and how to act in some situations. Then the code written by different programmers will be visually similar: it will be easier for you to work with someone else's code, and vice versa. There are also generally accepted coding standards and even programming platforms that will tell you how to follow these standards. This is important both when working in a team and if you write code alone.

2. Refactoring. This is a reworking of the program code without changing its behavior. That is, first you have a creative process: you write so that the program works, and then you “clean up after yourself”, put the code in order, to the desired look. As a bonus, refactoring helps to rethink the architectural decisions of the program.

3. Typing. Let's explain with a real example. Players wrote to the developers of one game about problems, and then suddenly stopped. At first, the developers were delighted: everything is so cool that there are no problems. Later it turned out that the username had changed to a nickname in the game, and because of this, the feedback form stopped working: complaints were simply not sent. Typing (declaring a data type) will help avoid such errors and simplify maintenance and refactoring.

4. Code review. This is when you and a colleague check each other's code and give each other feedback. This practice helps to avoid stupid mistakes and thoughtlessness. In addition (and this is also important), you are aware of what the other person is doing, and you do not have to duplicate functions. A wonderful bonus - this method will help to gently adapt beginners.

5. Automatic tests we will talk about them in a little more detail. These are simple programs that run code with different parameters and check against already known results. What are the tests?

  • Unit test individual parts of the program.

  • e2e test the system as a whole.

  • Loaders check how well the program handles loads.

  • Screenshots compare before and after screenshots and react to changes.

  • Others: mutational, penetration…

With tests, you really need to be careful so that it doesn’t turn out that you spend hours looking for an error in the code, but the problem is in the tests themselves. Therefore, it is useful to write tests first, and then take on the code itself, when you have a clear structure and understanding in your head of what needs to be done and what you want to get.

There are other ways to improve the code (for example, pair programming). The best results can be obtained if you use several methods at once: this is more reliable.

When good code is bad

We've been talking so much about how important quality code is now, and now we're saying the opposite? Yes. Sometimes good, "correct" code isn't all that great.
The reason is very simple: in programming, unfortunately, you have to choose between quality and speed of work, and in some cases speed is more important.

To understand how important speed is, imagine two developers: John and Steve. One is thorough, pedantic, and the second is its complete opposite. Both came up with the idea to make some kind of product. John sat down, came up with the architecture and started to do it well and slowly. And has been doing it for six months now. Steve did everything in a month, with errors, bugs - but immediately launched it, found customers, made the second release... As a result, six months later, when John had just completed his wonderful product, Steve already has many users and the name of the product is already well known. And he fixed some of the bugs in the second release.

Moral of the story: sometimes it's more important to get to the market faster and occupy a niche than to do it perfectly.

How then to be? Bad code is bad, good code is not always good either ... The point is this:

Code quality is a parameter that
we change depending on the goal.

If you are writing an application for a bank, then you will spend a lot of time testing and developing, because a mistake can cost hundreds of thousands of dollars.

If you are making a landing page that simply tells about a temporary promotion and collects emails into a database, then speed is already important here. You know that once the promotion ends, your code will no longer be needed. There is no need to refine and maintain it, and other developers will not use it either - it is enough to write quickly and check that it works, the rest is not important.

These are the basic principles of creating quality code: do not forget about standards, look back at what you have already written, do not be afraid of feedback from colleagues and do not forget to test. But at the very beginning, it is worth weighing: quality is important to you now, or you need to finish development as soon as possible.
item.author: vadimatamanenko

item.title: 初级开发人员必读的 4 本书 | 4 Must-Reads for the Junior Developer
item.description:
作为软件工程师的职业可能会令人困惑，如果您是新手，更是如此。 这里有 5 本书可以帮助理解这个复杂的问题。

此列表中唯一一本并非严格针对软件工程师的书。 一篇长而全面的读物，涵盖了许多对规划你的职业生涯有用的策略和技巧。 这本有声读物由 John Sonmez（作者）亲自讲述，在您的通勤途中很容易阅读。 这本书涵盖了您可能面临的许多问题，例如学习哪种语言，如何填补您的技术知识“较软”主题中的空白，例如面试时穿什么。

受到许多人的高度评价，并且是任何软件工程师阅读清单上最重要的建议之一。 我每隔几年就会重读这本书，它给我带来了新的见解，每次都证明它是一颗宝石。

虽然它不是最全面的，但它确实使它更有趣，特别是如果您没有计算机科学背景！ 我建议使用这本书作为对该主题更正式研究的介绍。

可能是我拥有的最长的一本书。 类似于The Complete Software Developer's Career Guide - John Sonmez的范围，但侧重于技术方面的求职以及如何为它做准备。 涵盖的主题包括面试后应该问的问题，以及重要数据结构和算法的应用。



原文:

A career as a Software Engineer can be confusing, moreso if you're new. Here are 5 books to help make sense of this complex problem.

1. So Good They Can't Ignore You - Cal Newport

The only book on this list not strictly catered for Software Engineers. Cal Newport writes about the mindset of taking control of your life, providing research-backed evidence for learning to love what you do.

2. The Complete Software Developer's Career Guide - John Sonmez

A long, but comprehensive read that covers many of the strategies and techniques useful to planning your career. The audio book was narrated by John Sonmez (the author) himself and is easy to consume on your commute. The book covers many questions you might face such as which language to learn, how to fill gaps in your technical knowledge "softer" topics such as what to wear to interviews.

3. The Pragmatic Programmer - David Thomas, Andrew Hunt

Viewed in high regard by many and one of the top recommendations on any Software Engineer's reading list. The Pragmatic Programmer highlights many common pitfalls and best practices that will help you approach technical challenges. I reread this book every few years and it brings me new insight proves to be a gem every time.

4. Grokking Algorithms

Grokking Algorithms is a very accessible way to learn data structures and algorithms. While it isn't the most comprehensive, it certainly makes it more enjoyable, especially if you do not have a background in Computer Science! I would recommend using this book as an introduction into more formal studies on the subject.

5. Cracking the Coding Interview

Probably the longest book I own. Similar to The Complete Software Developer's Career Guide - John Sonmez in scope, but with a focus on the technical aspect job hunt and how to prepare for it. Covers topics such as questions you should ask after an interview, and exerts of important data structures and algorithms.

item.title: [JP] go-retry - Go で处理をからリトライできるパッケージを作った | [JP] go-retry - Go で処理を途中からリトライできるパッケージを作った
item.description:
この记事は2021年11月にzenn.devで公开されたものです。

なぜ作ったのか

通信处理やインストール处理など、一连のを Step by step に実行してステップ毎にリトライさせることがあったので、その実装をパッケージとしたて切り。

最初はがさされるまでリトライリトライするの実装でしでしでしが，さすがさすがさすがさすがにに现在现在现在现在现在现在はははははははははははははははははははははははははははははは

使い方

使い方はretry.DoWithContextにリトライ间歇を设定するretry.DurationFuncを设定し、あとは実行したいをステップ毎に数化して渡すだけです。

 package main import ( "context" "log" "github.com/kenkyu392/go-retry" ) func main () { // キャンセル用にコンテキストを用意する。 ctx , cancel := context . WithCancel ( context . Background ()) defer cancel () // 処理を実行する。 errs := retry . DoWithContext ( ctx , // Exponential Backoff を行いながら 5 回リトライする。 retry . ExponentialBackoff ( 5 ), // 実行したい処理をステップ毎に関数に分離して渡す。 func ( ctx context . Context ) error { // リトライ不可能な何かが発生した場合は// retry.Canceled で処理をキャンセルする。 if failed { return retry . Canceled } return nil }, // 前のステップが成功したら次のステップに進む。 func ( ctx context . Context ) error { return nil }, ) // 実行中に発生したエラーを全て確認する。 for _ , err := range errs { log . Print ( err ) } }

指数退避 とは？

简単说明と处理の回数に合わせてリトライ间隔关数关数的的にに长く长く长くするするアルゴリズムです。。リトライリトライ间隔间隔をを长く长くするすることことのの必要必要必要必要性など详しく调べる调べる调べる调べるとしては「处理の冲突を避けるため」や「负担を軽减するため」に実装することが多いと思います。

リトライ间歇の调整

リトライ间歇の调整にはretry.DurationFuncを使用します。现在このパッケージには标准的なretry.ExponentialBackoffとretry.Durationが実装されています。

间隔调整关数にし理由は，単纯単纯にカスタムににするするするのののががががが好き好きななこともももありありますますががががretry.ExponentialBackoff返回200ms + (20ms ~ 100ms)ののランダム値値を使用たアルゴリズムでで実装されれていいますますますがが，，ランダムランダムななな値値値をををを使用使用使用し使用使用使用使用使用使用使用使用使用使用使用使用使用使用使用使用使用使用使用使用使用使用使用で简単に使用できます。

 func ExponentialBackoff ( maxRetries int ) retry . DurationFunc { return func ( retries int ) time . Duration { if maxRetries >= 0 && maxRetries < retries { return - 1 } return time . Duration ( math . Pow ( 2 , float64 ( retries )) * 100 ) * time . Millisecond } }

まとめ

パッケージはや处理，データベースなどの他にののインターフェースインターフェースインターフェースを伴う伴う伴う伴う伴う伴う伴うなどなどなどなどなどなど使用できるようようににに设计设计ししてて実装実装実装ししてていてててなななてみてください。

retry.DurationFuncの追加やご指摘などコントリビュートもお待ちしてます。 retry.DurationFuncの追加やご指摘などコントリビュートもお待ちしてます。



原文:

この記事は2021年11月に zenn.dev で公開されたものです。

なぜ作ったのか

通信処理やインストール処理など、一連の処理を Step by step に実行してステップ毎にリトライさせることがあったので、その実装をパッケージとして切り出してみました。

https://github.com/kenkyu392/go-retry

最初はとりあえずコンテキストがキャンセルされるまでリトライするだけの実装でしたが、さすがに現在は Exponential Backoff や最大リトライ回数など最低限必要なものを実装しています。

使い方

使い方は retry.DoWithContext にリトライ間隔を設定する retry.DurationFunc を設定し、あとは実行したい処理をステップ毎に関数化して渡すだけです。

package main import ( "context" "log" "github.com/kenkyu392/go-retry"
) func main() { // キャンセル用にコンテキストを用意する。 ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background()) defer cancel() // 処理を実行する。 errs := retry.DoWithContext(ctx, // Exponential Backoff を行いながら 5 回リトライする。 retry.ExponentialBackoff(5), // 実行したい処理をステップ毎に関数に分離して渡す。 func(ctx context.Context) error { // リトライ不可能な何かが発生した場合は // retry.Canceled で処理をキャンセルする。 if failed { return retry.Canceled } return nil }, // 前のステップが成功したら次のステップに進む。 func(ctx context.Context) error { return nil }, ) // 実行中に発生したエラーを全て確認する。 for _, err := range errs { log.Print(err) }
}

Exponential Backoff とは？

簡単に説明すると、処理の失敗回数に合わせてリトライ間隔を指数関数的に長くするアルゴリズムです。リトライ間隔を長くすることの必要性などは詳しく調べると良い説明が出てくると思いますが、用途としては「処理の衝突を避けるため」や「負荷を軽減するため」に実装することが多いと思います。

https://cloud.google.com/iot/docs/how-tos/exponential-backoff

https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/api-retries.html

リトライ間隔の調整

リトライ間隔の調整には retry.DurationFunc を使用します。現在このパッケージには標準的な retry.ExponentialBackoff と retry.Duration が実装されています。

リトライ間隔の調整を関数にした理由は、単純にカスタムできる実装にするのが好きなこともありますが、Exponential Backoff には様々な実装があることが大きいです。例えば、現在実装されている retry.ExponentialBackoff は 200ms + (20ms ~ 100ms) のようにランダムな値を使用したアルゴリズムで実装されていますが、ランダムな値を使用しない Exponential Backoff を使用したい場合は、以下のような独自関数を実装することで簡単に使用できます。

func ExponentialBackoff(maxRetries int) retry.DurationFunc { return func(retries int) time.Duration { if maxRetries >= 0 && maxRetries < retries { return -1 } return time.Duration(math.Pow(2, float64(retries))*100)*time.Millisecond }
}

まとめ

このパッケージは通信処理やインストール処理、データベースなどの他に対話型のインターフェースを伴う CLI などで使用できるように設計して実装しています。いろいろな場所に組み込みやすい設計にしているので、良ければ使ってみてください。

retry.DurationFunc の追加やご指摘などコントリビュートもお待ちしてます。

item.title: 30 天的 Leapfrog 学生伙伴计划学习挑战。 | 30 days of Leapfrog Student Partnership Program Learning Challenge.
item.description:
实际上，学习挑战是60天，但是我写这篇文章是因为我已经完成了一半的挑战。 挑战于 2022 年 4 月 14 日开始。挑战定义为标题，旨在学习与 IT 或技术相关的新事物或某些东西。

我对挑战感到兴奋，并且仍然要完成它。 在挑战的中途，我学习了 SOLID 原理、设计模式、微服务、figma 中的 UI 设计，阅读了关于区块链、加密货币、web3 等的文章。

此外，挑战需要我们在推特上发布当天的学习内容。
我每天都能发推文，直到挑战进行到一半，并期待着不间断地完成。

推特链接： aakash shakya



原文:

Actually, the learning challenge is of 60 days, but I am writing this article as I have completed the halfway to challenge. The challenge started on April 14, 2022. The challenge is as a title defined, to learn new or some thing related to the IT or tech stuff.

I was excited for the challenge and still am to complete it. During the halfway to the challenge, I learned about the SOLID principles, design patterns, Microservices, UI design in figma, read articles on blockchain, cryptocurrency, web3, and many more.

Also, the challenge needed us to tweet the learning of the day.
I have been able to tweet everyday till halfway of the challenge and looking forward to complete without break.
